name: What’s the difference between just having my business show up on Alexa and building an Alexa Skill for my company?

mainEntity.name: What’s the difference between just having my business show up on Alexa and building an Alexa Skill for my company?

acceptedAnswer.text:
Yes, there’s a meaningful difference: being “found” by Alexa as a local result comes from directory data, while an Alexa Skill is a custom voice app you build and publish.

Most small businesses only need to optimize listings; a Skill is useful when you want branded, interactive experiences.

Key factors:
- Directory-based presence powers “Alexa, find a plumber near me” type queries
- Alexa Skills require an Amazon developer account and a full skill setup (name, invocation, intents, testing, certification)
- Skills can answer FAQs, take bookings, or offer custom content under your brand
- Skills take more time and maintenance but give you more control over what Alexa says

Decide first whether you simply need to be discoverable or you want richer interactions; that determines whether you focus on listings only or invest in building a Skill.
Most local businesses start with listings and explore Skills later as their voice strategy matures.
acceptedAnswer.description: Showing up on Alexa as a local business comes from directory data (Google, Yelp, Bing Places, etc.), while an Alexa Skill is a custom voice app you build in the Alexa developer console. Listings get you found; a Skill lets you control branded, interactive experiences like FAQs or bookings.
